# Product Standards, Compliance, & Certifications
[ Learn More ](https://www.quintessencelabs.com/hubfs/PDFs/QuintessenceLabs-Product-Standards-Compliance-Certifications-Technical-Advisory.pdf)
## Common Criteria Certification
The Common Criteria (CC) consists of cybersecurity requirements that address encryption, auditing, security updates, and other related topics. The Network Device Collaborative Protection Profile (NDcPP) is a compilation of CC requirements specifically designed for network devices. Conformance with the NDcPP is verified through independent testing conducted at NIAP-accredited laboratories.  
  
The TSF is currently under evaluation for compliance with NDcPP – see <https://www.niap-ccevs.org/> products, group Products in Evaluation, VID 11518.
## Trusted Security Foundation® (TSF®) Key & Policy Manager
QuintessenceLabs’ TSF 400 key and policy manager generates, imports, and manages cryptographic keys for network-attached clients. Embedded within the TSF is an Entrust nShield XC FIPS 140-3 Level 3 validated Hardware Security Module (HSM). The HSM serves as a root of trust ensuring the protection of sensitive data and facilitating cryptographic operations.
## The HSM Provides FIPS 140 Approved Protection
Use of the integrated HSM is conformant with the FIPS 140-3 Security Policy for the HSM.
### Protection of Managed Key Material
Client keys can be either generated on the TSF, utilizing the NIST SP 800-90B entropy source, or imported into the TSF over a secure channel.
In both scenarios, the keys are securely wrapped within the HSM using a wrapping key that is generated within the HSM.
### TLS Handshake
TLS provides secure channels for client-server, server-server, and admin-server communications. Server-side TLS private keys are generated, stored, and used within the HSM.
TLS cryptographic handshake operations are performed inside the HSM.
### Internal Public Key Infrastructure
The TSF includes an embedded Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) service. The local private Certificate Authority (CA) key is generated within the HSM. All operations performed using the private key are  
performed within the HSM. This includes the creation of PKI credentials on behalf of external users, the creation of PKI credentials for internal use, and certificate signing operations performed within  
the HSM.  
  
Use of the integrated HSM is conformant with the FIPS 140-3 Security Policy for the HSM.
### NIST SP  
800-57
The Trusted Security Foundation Key & Policy Management application is 100% compliant with NIST Special Publication 800-57, where applicable.
Key Management | CSRC [(nist.gov)](http://nist.gov) - NIST Special Publication 800-57 provides cryptographic key management guidance. It consists of three parts. Part 1 provides general guidance and best practices for the management of cryptographic keying material. Part 2 provides guidance on policy and security planning requirements for U.S. government agencies. Finally, Part 3 provides guidance when using the cryptographic features of current systems.
[ Learn More ](https://csrc.nist.gov/Projects/Key-Management/Key-Management-Guidelines)
### Commercial Solutions for Classified (CSfC)
The Commercial Solutions for Classified (CSfC) program is a key component of NSA’s commercial cybersecurity strategy aimed at providing secure cybersecurity solutions leveraging commercial technologies and products. Established to enable the use of commercial products in layered solutions, NSA’s CSfC Program protects classified National Security Systems (NSS) data.
The TSF 400 has been reviewed by NSA validating conformity with the CSfC KGS Approval Criteria and the Symmetric Key Management (KM) Requirements Annex for the use of Symmetric Pre-Shared Keys to deliver quantum-resistant cryptographic protection for classified information within properly configured, maintained, and monitored CSfC solutions.
KGS solutions meeting the CSfC KGS Approval Criteria and the Symmetric Key Management (KM) Requirements Annex must also obtain approval for use through the CSfC solution registration process.
[ Learn More ](https://csrc.nist.gov/Projects/Key-Management/Key-Management-Guidelines)
### VMware by Broadcom Certification
The Trusted Security Foundation® Key & Policy Management application is a VMware by Broadcom Certified Key Management Server (KMS).
KMS Compatibility Guide ([https://compatibilityguide.broadcom.com/](https://compatibilityguide.broadcom.com/)
The KMS listed have passed VMware’s KMS Certification tests, which allows these certified KMS to provide a measure of reliability and stability of the end solution in customer deployments. All the tests contained in the KMS Certification plugin are meant to verify that the vendor’s KMIP-compliant KMS works with the vSphere VM Encryption feature.
VMware by Broadcom is also an OEM partner of QuintessenceLabs, licensing the qClient KMIP Client for the vSphere VM Encryption feature.
[ Learn More ](https://www.vmware.com/resources/compatibility/pdf/vi_kms_guide.pdf)
## qStreamTM Quantum Random Number Generator (QRNG)
### NIST SP 800-90B CERTIFIED
Entropy Certificate #E145 – [Cryptographic Module Validation Program | CSRC](https://csrc.nist.gov/projects/cryptographic-module-validation-program/entropy-validations/certificate/145)
Leidos performed an independent lab evaluation of qStream™ for compliance with NIST SP 800-90A and SP 800-90B. Part of that work included validation of correctness of cryptographic algorithm implementation against the NIST CAVP.
_Cryptographic Algorithm Validation Program | CSRC ([nist.gov](http://nist.gov))_
![qStream2-1](https://www.quintessencelabs.com/hs-fs/hubfs/qStream2-1.png?width=800&height=700&name=qStream2-1.png)
## Dieharder Test
qStream™ QRNG is a Leidos validated True Random Number Generator, making use of the Dieharder Tests, which include not only the dieharder tests but other tests from the NIST statistical test suite and others.
Dieharder Tests are a battery of statistical tests for measuring the quality of a random number generator. They include Birthday spacings test, Overlapping 5-permutation test, Binary rank matrices test, Bitstream test, OPSO, OQSO & DNA test, Count-the-1’s test, Parking lot test, Minimum distance test, 3D spheres test, Squeeze test, Overlapping sums test, Runs test, Craps test.
[Learn More](https://webhome.phy.duke.edu/~rgb/General/dieharder.php)
![Dieharder Test](https://www.quintessencelabs.com/hs-fs/hubfs/Dieharder%20Test.png?width=800&height=700&name=Dieharder%20Test.png)
##  JOSCAR-AU Accredited Supplier 
QuintessenceLabs is a fully registered member of the **Joint Supply Chain Accreditation Register (JOSCAR-AU)**. This accreditation is a cross-sector collaborative solution used by the defense, aerospace, and security industries to identify "fit-for-business" suppliers.
Our successful registration demonstrates that QuintessenceLabs meets the highest industry standards for financial stability, technical capability, and information security. This certification enables major prime contractors to confidently partner with us, knowing our processes have been rigorously vetted to minimize supply chain risk and streamline procurement.
![JOSCAR cert badge](https://www.quintessencelabs.com/hs-fs/hubfs/JOSCAR%20cert%20badge.png?width=2600&height=1560&name=JOSCAR%20cert%20badge.png)

              <div class="cover-card__text clear small-text">
                <h3>Protection of Managed Key Material</h3>
<p>Client keys can be either generated on the TSF, utilizing the NIST SP 800-90B entropy source, or imported into the TSF over a secure channel.</p>
<p>In both scenarios, the keys are securely wrapped within the HSM using a wrapping key that is generated within the HSM.</p>

            <div class="cover-card__btn">

          <div class="cover-card__overlay" style="opacity: 0.00;"></div>
          
            
          
          <div class="cover-card__labels">
            <!--
            -->
          </div>
          <div class="cover-card__content">
            
              <div class="cover-card__text clear small-text">
                <h3>TLS Handshake</h3>
<p>TLS provides secure channels for client-server, server-server, and admin-server communications. Server-side TLS private keys are generated, stored, and used within the HSM.</p>
<p>TLS cryptographic handshake operations are performed inside the HSM.</p>
              </div>
            
            <div class="cover-card__btn">

              <div class="cover-card__text clear small-text">
                <h3>Internal Public Key Infrastructure</h3>
<p>The TSF includes an embedded Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) service. The local private Certificate Authority (CA) key is generated within the HSM. All operations performed using the private key are<br>performed within the HSM. This includes the creation of PKI credentials on behalf of external users, the creation of PKI credentials for internal use, and certificate signing operations performed within<br>the HSM.<br><br>Use of the integrated HSM is conformant with the FIPS 140-3 Security Policy for the HSM.</p>

    <div class="rtext__content clear normal-text">
      <h3 style="text-align: left;">NIST SP<br>800-57</h3>
<div style="text-align: left;">The Trusted Security Foundation Key & Policy Management application is 100% compliant with NIST Special Publication 800-57, where applicable.</div>
<div style="text-align: left;"> </div>
<div style="text-align: left;">Key Management | CSRC <a href="http://nist.gov" rel="noopener" target="_blank">(nist.gov)</a> - NIST Special Publication 800-57 provides cryptographic key management guidance. It consists of three parts. Part 1 provides general guidance and best practices for the management of cryptographic keying material. Part 2 provides guidance on policy and security planning requirements for U.S. government agencies. Finally, Part 3 provides guidance when using the cryptographic features of current systems.</div>
<div style="text-align: center;"> </div>

      <h3 style="text-align: left;">Commercial Solutions for Classified (CSfC)</h3>
<div style="text-align: left;">The Commercial Solutions for Classified (CSfC) program is a key component of NSA’s commercial cybersecurity strategy aimed at providing secure cybersecurity solutions leveraging commercial technologies and products. Established to enable the use of commercial products in layered solutions, NSA’s CSfC Program protects classified National Security Systems (NSS) data.</div>
<div style="text-align: left;"> </div>
<div style="text-align: left;">The TSF 400 has been reviewed by NSA validating conformity with the CSfC KGS Approval Criteria and the Symmetric Key Management (KM) Requirements Annex for the use of Symmetric Pre-Shared Keys to deliver quantum-resistant cryptographic protection for classified information within properly configured, maintained, and monitored CSfC solutions.</div>
<div style="text-align: left;"> </div>
<div style="text-align: left;">KGS solutions meeting the CSfC KGS Approval Criteria and the Symmetric Key Management (KM) Requirements Annex must also obtain approval for use through the CSfC solution registration process.</div>
<div> </div>
    </div>
